title: gagea pratensis f. angustifolia
intro: Gagea pratensis f. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved marsh cress or narrow-leaved gagea, is a flowering plant that belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Gagea pratensis f. angustifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its potential to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties. Gagea pratensis f. angustifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible medicinal resource.
